title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: West German Browning Hi-Power.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: The Browning Hi Power is a single-action, semi-automatic handgun available in 9mm and .40 S&W calibers.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: It is Axel Foley 's main sidearm.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: History and Description [ ] It is based on a design by American firearms inventor John Browning, and completed by Dieudonné Saive at Fabrique Nationale (FN) of Herstal, Belgium.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: Browning died in 1926, several years before the design was finalized.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: The Hi-Power is one of the most widely used military pistols in history, having been used by the armed forces of over 50 countries.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: The Hi Power name is somewhat misleading and alluded to the 13-round magazine capacity; almost twice that of contemporary designs such as the Luger P08 or Mauser 1910.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: The Browning was one of the first, if not the first, pistols to utilize a double-stack magazine with ten or thirteen round capacities, thus making it more high-powered than pistols with single-stack magazines, like the 1911, which is what the Hi-Power is partly based off of.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: However, despite the Hi-Power being a single-action firearm, it had a heavy trigger pull, almost ten pounds, which is far heavier than that of the 1911 , which was less than four pounds.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: The pistol is often referred to as an HP (for "Hi-Power" or "High-Power") or as a GP (for the French term, "Grande Puissance").

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: The terms P-35 and HP-35 are also used, based on the introduction of the pistol in 1935.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: It is most often called the "Hi Power", even in Belgium.

title: Browning Hi-Power
sentence_text: Other names include BAP (Browning Automatic Pistol), particularly in Irish service, or BHP (Browning High-Power).

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: 2) The Glock 17 is an Austrian striker-fired semi-automatic pistol chambered in 9x19mm.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: It is used by police and armies around the world and is famous for its 17 round polymer-coated magazine.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: It was used in Beverly Hills Cop III as Ellis DeWald's main weapon.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Description [ ] History and Design [ ] The Glock 17 was introduced in 1982.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Its inventor, Gaston Glock, was an Austrian engineer who only had experience with synthetic polymers, not firearms.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: He later got the idea to make a simply constructed pistol almost entirely out of polymers to compete for the spot in the Austrian military pistol trials to replace the aging Walther P38.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: However, the Glock 17 is not the first polymer handgun.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Heckler and Koch of Germany twenty years earlier made the VP70 to little success.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Glock got together a team of firearms experts from across Europe to determine the most desirable traits in a handgun, designed around the criteria, which included that all actions necessary to prepare the pistol for firing must be done single-handed or ambidextrous, disassembly of the main parts must not require tools, the pistol's construction may not exceed 58 individual parts (equivalent of a P38, although Glocks have 34) and after firing 15,000 rounds of standard ammunition, the pistol will fire an overpressure test cartridge.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: The Glock's frame, magazine body and several other components are made from a high-strength nylon-based radio-opaque polymer invented by Gaston Glock and called Polymer 2.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: This plastic was specially formulated to provide increased durability and is more resilient than carbon steel and most steel alloys.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Polymer 2 is resistant to shock, caustic liquids and temperature extremes where traditional steel/alloy frames would warp and become brittle.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: The injection molded frame's trigger guard is squared off at the front and checkered.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: The grip has a non-slip, stippled surface checkering on the sides and both the front and rear straps.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: The Glock 17 outperformed 8 different pistols from five other established manufacturers (Heckler & Koch of Germany offered their P7M8, P7M13 and P9S, SIG Sauer of Germany bid with their P220 and P226 models, Beretta of Italy submitted their model 92SB-F, FN Herstal proposed an updated variant of the Browning Hi-Power and the home-grown Steyr entered the competition with the GB).

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Glock later emerged as the winner of all 17 of the grueling endurance tests and was quickly adopted into service with the Austrian military and police forces in 1982 as the P80 (Pistole 80), with an initial order for 25,000 guns.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Popularity [ ] Although the Glock Model 17 is noted for its 17-round magazine, that is not why it is called the Glock 17.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: It is so named because the final design of the Glock 17 was Gaston Glock's seventeenth patent.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Oddly enough, the Glock 17 also met all 17 requirements for the Austrian military trials.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Although the Glock was mocked for its blocky appearance, sights and controls, lack of hammer, lack of external safety and polymer parts and for years there was a bias against polymer guns on the market due to fears that they would not be detected in metal detectors.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: This fear was later proven to be untrue but nonetheless was featured in Die Hard 2 (which just so happened to be the first movie of many to feature a Glock).

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: The results of the Austrian trials sparked interest in the United States, where a similar effort to select a service-wide replacement for the M1911 had been ongoing since the late 1970s (known as the Joint Service Small Arms Program).

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: In late 1983, the United States Department of Defense inquired about the Glock pistol and received four samples of the Glock 17 for unofficial evaluation.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Glock was then invited to participate in the XM9 Personal Defense Pistol Trials, but declined because the DOD specifications would require extensive retooling of production equipment and providing 35 test samples in an unrealistic time frame.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Shortly thereafter, the Glock 17 was accepted into service with the Norwegian, Swiss, German, French, Russian, British and Swedish Armed Forces, surpassing all prior NATO durability standards.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: By 1992, some 350,000 pistols had been sold in more than 45 countries, including 250,000 in the United States alone.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Today it is one of the easiest pistols to disassemble and the Glock is unique in the way that the milled slides can easily be switched from pistol to pistol to allow for different caliber changes.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: It is also the only pistol that has three separate internal safeties that engage at once.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: The Glock 17 is one of the only three Glock models to be manufactured in all four generations.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: There are three safeties on the gun (trigger safety, drop safety and firing pin block), and since it is technically a light double action trigger as opposed to a hammer-fired designation such as DA/SA, the Glock is known as the "safe action pistol".

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Glocks today are used by sport shooters, law enforcement, numerous armies across the globe and civilian defense.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Over 7 million Glocks have been produced as of 2007.

title: Glock 17
sentence_text: Since 1982, the number of Glock model patents has increased from 17 to 42 in several different calibers.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: S&W 639 The Smith and Wesson Model 39 was a first-generation semi-automatic pistol developed for the US Army service pistol trials of 1954.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: After the Army abandoned its search for a new pistol, the Model 39 went on the civilian market in 1955 and was the first of Smith & Wesson's first generation semi-automatic pistols.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: Used by the Illinois State Police and the US Navy SEALs, it was a groundbreaking pistol for use with the military and law-enforcement agencies.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The S&W 639 (the stainless, second-generation version of the S&W 39) was seen in Beverly Hills Cop .

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: Overview [ ] The Smith and Wesson Model 39 was the first U.S.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: designed double action (DA) semi-automatic pistol marketed in the United States.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The German Walther P38 DA pistol impressed American ordnance personnel during World War II.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Army Ordnance Corps issued a proposal for an American equivalent to the P38.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: In 1949, Smith and Wesson began development of the Model 39 DA semi-auto in caliber 9x19mm Luger.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: Smith and Wesson began selling the Model 39 commercially in 1955.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: This is considered a first generation pistol.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: Since the Model 39 came out, S&W has continuously developed the design into its third generation pistols now on the market.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: First generation models use a 2 digit model number, second generation use 3 digits, and third generation models use 4 digits.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 39 was originally manufactured with an anodized aluminum frame, a curved backstrap and a blued carbon steel slide that carried the manual safety.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The grip was of three pieces made of two walnut wood panels joined by a metal backstrap.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: It has a magazine release located at the rear of the trigger guard, similar to the M1911A1 it was designed to replace.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 39 was produced in limited numbers with a steel frame.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: This steel-frame model provided the basis for the Model 52, one of the outstanding target pistols of the day.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 52 had a longer (5 inch) barrel and slide and was one of the few semi-automatic pistols ever chambered for the .38 Special cartridge (with flush-seated, full wadcutter bullets only).

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The shape of the rimmed cartridge limited the magazine capacity to five rounds.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: A further variant, the Model 952, in 9mm Para , is still produced in limited quantities by Smith & Wesson's Performance Center.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 52 was discontinued in 1992 when the machinery to manufacture the pistol broke down and it was deemed too costly to replace.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 39 was the basis for the later Smith & Wesson Model 59, retaining the original 9mm Parabellum caliber, but incorporating a wider aluminum frame with a straight backstrap to accommodate a double-column magazine that could hold 14 rounds.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 39 used a conventional slide, barrel bushing, slide arrangement as opposed to the exposed barrel arrangement of the P38.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: Model 39 locking is done by a modification of the Browning P35 (High Power) cam-locked breech.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: First generation Model 39 slides used either a long, spring steel extractor or a spring-loaded, pivoting claw extractor.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: Long extractors tended to be fragile and were replaced by the pivoting type.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: However, the long extractor proved to be more reliable than the pivoting version.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 39 employed many features common to the Walther P38 such as a decocking safety that disconnected the trigger and hammer.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: Smith & Wesson even copied the 8-round single-stack magazine as well but added a magazine catch cutout to it.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The overall length of the Model 39 was 7.6 inches, the barrel is 4 inches long.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The weight of the Model 39 was 1.72 pounds; this light weight is due to its aluminum frame.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Illinois State Police adopted the Model 39 in 1967, an action which helped ingratiate semi-automatic pistols with law enforcement.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: This publicity helped commercial sales and set the stage for the more acceptable Model 59 with its high capacity magazine at least in undercover or detective police use where the DA feature (and the larger magazine capacity) was considered by many to be superior to single-stack semi-automatics and revolvers of the time.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: A modified version of he Model 39 with a wider grip frame to accommodate a 14-round magazine was used by Naval Special Warfare units during the Vietnam War.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: It was issued to officers-in-charge (OICs) of MST-2 detachments as their sidearm.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: SEAL Teams used either the Model 39 without modification or a modified version, the Mk 22 Mod 0, which was called the "hush puppy".

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The modified pistol had a suppressor with a slide lock (which kept the slide from moving backwards upon firing, thereby decreasing produced noise) The Mk 22 Mod 0 had raised iron sights, to provide easier sighting over a bulky suppressor.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The gun's purpose was to eliminate sentry dogs or guards without alarming the main target.

title: Smith & Wesson 639
sentence_text: The Model 39 was later modified into the ASP-9 pistol.

sentence_text: As a child actor under contract to Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer he first came to the public's attention in films such as Anchors Aweigh and The Green Years; as a young adult he played a lead role in the 1957 Broadway and 1959 screen adaptations of Meyer Levin's Compulsion, a novel based on the true-life story of Leopold and Loeb.

sentence_text: Stockwell has been widely reported to be the godfather of actress Amber Tamblyn; however, in a 2009 interview with Parade Magazine, Tamblyn explained that Stockwell was one of three famous friends of her father, actor Russ Tamblyn, who were always around the house when she was growing up, and who were big influences on her life.

title: Dean Stockwell
sentence_text: The word "godfather" was "just a loose term I’ve always used for all of them," Tamblyn explained in the interview, suggesting that Stockwell's "godfather" moniker is informal, at best.

title: Glenn Frey

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: Biography [ ] Frey played guitar with the Eagles as well as piano and keyboards and sang lead vocals on many Eagles hits such as "Take It Easy", "Peaceful Easy Feeling", "Tequila Sunrise", "Already Gone", "Lyin' Eyes", "New Kid in Town", and "Heartache Tonight".

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: After the breakup of the Eagles in 1980, Frey embarked on a moderately successful solo career.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: He released his debut album, No Fun Aloud in 1982 and went on to record Top 40 hits "The One You Love", "Smuggler's Blues", "Sexy Girl", "The Heat Is On", "You Belong to the City", "True Love", "Soul Searchin'", and "Livin' Right".

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: As a member of the Eagles, Frey won six Grammy Awards, and five American Music Awards.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: The Eagles have sold over 120 million albums worldwide and were inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 1998, the first year they were nominated.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: As a solo artist and with the Eagles combined, Frey has released 24 Top 40 singles on the Billboard Hot 100.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: Frey was born in Detroit, Michigan.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: Growing up in Royal Oak, Michigan, he studied the keyboards with concert pianist John Harrison and became part of the mid-1960s Detroit rock scene.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: One of his earliest bands was called the Subterraneans, named after Jack Kerouac's novel, and included fellow Dondero High School Class of '66 students Doug Edwards (later replaced by Lenny Mintz) on drums, Doug Gunsch and Bill Barnes on guitar and Jeff Hodge on bass.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: After graduating from high school in 1966, Frey played for a while with the local band The Four of Us, modeled after The Byrds.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: In 1967 he formed the Mushrooms with Jeff Burrows, Bill Barnes, Doug Gunch and Larry Mintz.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: The group scored a coup in getting Bob Seger to write their first single and made television appearances to promote it.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: In the later part of 1967, Frey pulled together another band called Heavy Metal Kids with Jeff Burrows, Jeff Alborell, Paul Kelcourse and Lance Dickerson.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: His first professional recording experience, at age 19, was performing acoustic guitar and background vocals on Bob Seger's single, "Ramblin' Gamblin' Man" in 1968.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: Frey has said that Seger strongly encouraged and influenced him to focus on writing his own original songs.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: Frey and Seger remained good friends and occasional songwriting partners in later years.

title: Glenn Frey

title: Glenn Frey

title: Glenn Frey

title: Glenn Frey

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: Frey wrote or co-wrote (often with Henley) many of the group's songs, and sang the lead vocals on a number of Eagles hits including "Take It Easy", "Peaceful Easy Feeling", "Already Gone", "Tequila Sunrise", "Lyin' Eyes", "New Kid in Town", "Heartache Tonight", and "How Long".

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: The Eagles broke up in 1980 and reunited in 1994, when they released a new album titled Hell Freezes Over.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: In 2012 on the Tavis Smiley Show, Frey told Smiley, "When the Eagles broke up, people used to ask me and Don, "When are the Eagles getting back together?' We used to answer, 'When Hell freezes over.' We thought it was a pretty good joke.

title: Glenn Frey

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: We had a lot of fun.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: We had a lot more fun than I think people realize." The Eagles' album Long Road out of Eden was released in 2007, and Glenn Frey participated in the Eagles' The Long Road out of Eden Tour (2008–2011).

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: In 2013, the two-part documentary History of the Eagles, directed by Alison Ellwood and co-produced by Academy Award winner Alex Gibney, was aired on Showtime.

title: Glenn Frey
sentence_text: The authorised documentary caused a stir among fans, since it reportedly "shines a surprisingly harsh light on Glenn Frey and Don Henley, the group's chief songwriters and self-appointed leaders," and includes footage demonstrating that "Frey and Henley [were] dictators who berate their bandmates." The band's former guitarist Don Felder later criticized the film, saying "I didn't think it was really an accurate documentary", though this appears to be in reference to what the film either omitted or glossed over more than any personal issue.

title: Carlotta
sentence_text: History [ ] Beverly Hills Cop [ ] Carlotta was sent by his superiors and met with an undercover Axel Foley in order to purchase stolen cigarettes.

title: Carlotta
sentence_text: While there, he tried to purchase the truck filled with illegal cigarettes for $2,000 instead of the already agreed upon $5,000.

title: Carlotta
sentence_text: Carlotta tried to trick Foley by saying that there was a mistake and that they had stiffed him on the extra $3,000, asking Axel to take the $2,000 and promised that he would make up for it in the future with another delivery from Axel.

title: Carlotta
sentence_text: Knowing $2,000 was not enough to accept, Foley continued to pressure Carlotta for the full $5,000 when they were suddenly interrupted by a patrolling police cruiser.

title: Carlotta
sentence_text: After Foley tried to trick the officers, they demanded to see both Carlotta and Foley's ID, resulting in Carlotta running away.

title: Carlotta
sentence_text: Carlotta's partner quickly drove the truck away with Axel still inside the back, while Carlotta was chased by one of the officers.

title: Carlotta
sentence_text: The other officer ran back into his police cruiser and chased after Carlotta's partner and Foley in the cigarette truck, resulting in a massive police chase which ended in tons of property damages and the eventual capture of Carlotta's partner, although Carlotta himself escaped.

title: Carlotta
sentence_text: Beverly Hills Cop II [ ] Two years following the cigarette deal gone bad, Carlotta attended a deal with his Uncle Vinnie .

title: Carlotta
sentence_text: Once again Axel was undercover trying to purchase blank American Express black credit cards from Vinnie.

title: Carlotta
sentence_text: During the deal, Carlotta immediately recognized Axel as the same man from the cigarette truck incident.

title: Carlotta
sentence_text: The two of them got into a heated argument, with Axel claiming that Carlotta was an undercover cop to which Vinnie did not believe, stating Carlotta was his nephew.

title: Carlotta
sentence_text: Axel continued to claim that Carlotta was an undercover officer, to which Carlotta took offense to and explained that his friend was caught and now serving time in jail for the police chase.

title: Carlotta
sentence_text: Vinnie tried to continue the deal but Foley cut it off, claiming he would not do business with Carlotta again and would only do business with Vinnie alone.

title: Carlotta
sentence_text: With that, Foley stormed off from the deal leaving Carlotta angrily shouting at him as he left.
